data.title: The Market Doesn't Like What It Sees From Cosmos Health Inc.'s (NASDAQ:COSM) Revenues Yet As Shares Tumble 42%
data.description:
Cosmos Health Inc. (NASDAQ:COSM) shares have had a horrible month, losing 42% after a relatively good period beforehand. Longer-term, the stock has been solid despite a difficult 30 days, gaining 15% in the last year.

Following the heavy fall in price, it would be understandable if you think Cosmos Health is a stock with good investment prospects with a price-to-sales ratios (or "P/S") of 0.4x, considering almost half the companies in the United States' Healthcare industry have P/S ratios above 1.3x. Although, it's not wise to just take the P/S at face value as there may be an explanation why it's limited.

We've found 21 US stocks that are forecast to pay a dividend yield of over 6% next year. See the full list for free.

See our latest analysis for Cosmos Health

Advertisement

What Does Cosmos Health's Recent Performance Look Like?

For example, consider that Cosmos Health's financial performance has been poor lately as its revenue has been in decline. One possibility is that the P/S is low because investors think the company won't do enough to avoid underperforming the broader industry in the near future. However, if this doesn't eventuate then existing shareholders may be feeling optimistic about the future direction of the share price.

Although there are no analyst estimates available for Cosmos Health, take a look at this free data-rich visualisation to see how the company stacks up on earnings, revenue and cash flow.

Do Revenue Forecasts Match The Low P/S Ratio?

Cosmos Health's P/S ratio would be typical for a company that's only expected to deliver limited growth, and importantly, perform worse than the industry.

In reviewing the last year of financials, we were disheartened to see the company's revenues fell to the tune of 2.4%. As a result, revenue from three years ago have also fallen 1.7% overall. Therefore, it's fair to say the revenue growth recently has been undesirable for the company.

Comparing that to the industry, which is predicted to deliver 6.5% growth in the next 12 months, the company's downward momentum based on recent medium-term revenue results is a sobering picture.

With this information, we are not surprised that Cosmos Health is trading at a P/S lower than the industry. However, we think shrinking revenues are unlikely to lead to a stable P/S over the longer term, which could set up shareholders for future disappointment. Even just maintaining these prices could be difficult to achieve as recent revenue trends are already weighing down the shares.

The Final Word

Cosmos Health's P/S has taken a dip along with its share price. We'd say the price-to-sales ratio's power isn't primarily as a valuation instrument but rather to gauge current investor sentiment and future expectations.

As we suspected, our examination of Cosmos Health revealed its shrinking revenue over the medium-term is contributing to its low P/S, given the industry is set to grow. Right now shareholders are accepting the low P/S as they concede future revenue probably won't provide any pleasant surprises either. If recent medium-term revenue trends continue, it's hard to see the share price moving strongly in either direction in the near future under these circumstances.

It is also worth noting that we have found 4 warning signs for Cosmos Health (2 are a bit unpleasant!) that you need to take into consideration.

If you're unsure about the strength of Cosmos Health's business, why not explore our interactive list of stocks with solid business fundamentals for some other companies you may have missed.

data.title: Impressive Earnings May Not Tell The Whole Story For Exchange Income (TSE:EIF)
data.description:
Exchange Income Corporation's (TSE:EIF) robust earnings report didn't manage to move the market for its stock. Our analysis suggests that shareholders have noticed something concerning in the numbers.

This technology could replace computers: discover the 20 stocks are working to make quantum computing a reality.

In order to understand the potential for per share returns, it is essential to consider how much a company is diluting shareholders. In fact, Exchange Income increased the number of shares on issue by 14% over the last twelve months by issuing new shares. Therefore, each share now receives a smaller portion of profit. Per share metrics like EPS help us understand how much actual shareholders are benefitting from the company's profits, while the net income level gives us a better view of the company's absolute size. Check out Exchange Income's historical EPS growth by clicking on this link.

Advertisement

How Is Dilution Impacting Exchange Income's Earnings Per Share (EPS)?

As you can see above, Exchange Income has been growing its net income over the last few years, with an annualized gain of 36% over three years. In comparison, earnings per share only gained 6.0% over the same period. And over the last 12 months, the company grew its profit by 18%. But in comparison, EPS only increased by 10% over the same period. Therefore, the dilution is having a noteworthy influence on shareholder returns.

In the long term, earnings per share growth should beget share price growth. So it will certainly be a positive for shareholders if Exchange Income can grow EPS persistently. However, if its profit increases while its earnings per share stay flat (or even fall) then shareholders might not see much benefit. For the ordinary retail shareholder, EPS is a great measure to check your hypothetical "share" of the company's profit.

That might leave you wondering what analysts are forecasting in terms of future profitability. Luckily, you can click here to see an interactive graph depicting future profitability, based on their estimates.

Our Take On Exchange Income's Profit Performance

Each Exchange Income share now gets a meaningfully smaller slice of its overall profit, due to dilution of existing shareholders. Because of this, we think that it may be that Exchange Income's statutory profits are better than its underlying earnings power. Nonetheless, it's still worth noting that its earnings per share have grown at 6.0% over the last three years. Of course, we've only just scratched the surface when it comes to analysing its earnings; one could also consider margins, forecast growth, and return on investment, among other factors. If you want to do dive deeper into Exchange Income, you'd also look into what risks it is currently facing. While conducting our analysis, we found that Exchange Income has 2 warning signs and it would be unwise to ignore these bad boys.

Today we've zoomed in on a single data point to better understand the nature of Exchange Income's profit. But there is always more to discover if you are capable of focussing your mind on minutiae. data.title: Kelly Services (NASDAQ:KELY.A) Has Announced A Dividend Of $0.075
data.description:
Kelly Services, Inc. (NASDAQ:KELY.A) has announced that it will pay a dividend of $0.075 per share on the 3rd of December. This means the annual payment is 3.6% of the current stock price, which is above the average for the industry.

While the dividend yield is important for income investors, it is also important to consider any large share price moves, as this will generally outweigh any gains from distributions. Kelly Services' stock price has reduced by 41% in the last 3 months, which is not ideal for investors and can explain a sharp increase in the dividend yield.

AI is about to change healthcare. These 20 stocks are working on everything from early diagnostics to drug discovery. The best part - they are all under $10bn in marketcap - there is still time to get in early.

Advertisement

Kelly Services' Future Dividend Projections Seem Positive

We like to see robust dividend yields, but that doesn't matter if the payment isn't sustainable. Even though Kelly Services isn't generating a profit, it is generating healthy free cash flows that easily cover the dividend. In general, cash flows are more important than the more traditional measures of profit so we feel pretty comfortable with the dividend at this level.

The next year is set to see EPS grow by 124.7%. If the dividend continues along recent trends, we estimate the payout ratio will be 28%, which is in the range that makes us comfortable with the sustainability of the dividend.

View our latest analysis for Kelly Services

Dividend Volatility

Although the company has a long dividend history, it has been cut at least once in the last 10 years. Since 2015, the dividend has gone from $0.20 total annually to $0.30. This works out to be a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of approximately 4.1% a year over that time. We're glad to see the dividend has risen, but with a limited rate of growth and fluctuations in the payments the total shareholder return may be limited.

Dividend Growth Potential Is Shaky

Growing earnings per share could be a mitigating factor when considering the past fluctuations in the dividend. Over the past five years, it looks as though Kelly Services' EPS has declined at around 26% a year. Such rapid declines definitely have the potential to constrain dividend payments if the trend continues into the future. It's not all bad news though, as the earnings are predicted to rise over the next 12 months - we would just be a bit cautious until this becomes a long term trend.

The Dividend Could Prove To Be Unreliable

Overall, we don't think this company makes a great dividend stock, even though the dividend wasn't cut this year. In the past, the payments have been unstable, but over the short term the dividend could be reliable, with the company generating enough cash to cover it. We would probably look elsewhere for an income investment.

Market movements attest to how highly valued a consistent dividend policy is compared to one which is more unpredictable. However, there are other things to consider for investors when analysing stock performance. Taking the debate a bit further, we've identified 2 warning signs for Kelly Services that investors need to be conscious of moving forward. Looking for more high-yielding dividend ideas? Try our collection of strong dividend payers.

data.title: Positive Sentiment Still Eludes Hudson Technologies, Inc. (NASDAQ:HDSN) Following 26% Share Price Slump
data.description:
The Hudson Technologies, Inc. (NASDAQ:HDSN) share price has fared very poorly over the last month, falling by a substantial 26%. Longer-term, the stock has been solid despite a difficult 30 days, gaining 14% in the last year.

In spite of the heavy fall in price, given about half the companies in the United States have price-to-earnings ratios (or "P/E's") above 19x, you may still consider Hudson Technologies as an attractive investment with its 13x P/E ratio. Hudson Technologies hasn't been tracking well recently as its declining earnings compare poorly to other companies, which have seen some growth on average. Does Growth Match The Low P/E?

Hudson Technologies' P/E ratio would be typical for a company that's only expected to deliver limited growth, and importantly, perform worse than the market.

Retrospectively, the last year delivered a frustrating 24% decrease to the company's bottom line. This means it has also seen a slide in earnings over the longer-term as EPS is down 78% in total over the last three years. So unfortunately, we have to acknowledge that the company has not done a great job of growing earnings over that time.

Turning to the outlook, the next year should generate growth of 15% as estimated by the four analysts watching the company. Meanwhile, the rest of the market is forecast to expand by 16%, which is not materially different.

In light of this, it's peculiar that Hudson Technologies' P/E sits below the majority of other companies. Apparently some shareholders are doubtful of the forecasts and have been accepting lower selling prices.

Hudson Technologies' P/E has taken a tumble along with its share price. Typically, we'd caution against reading too much into price-to-earnings ratios when settling on investment decisions, though it can reveal plenty about what other market participants think about the company.

Right now shareholders are comfortable with the P/S as they are quite confident revenue aren't under threat. Unless the recent medium-term conditions change, they will continue to provide strong support to the share price.

data.title: Is BP’s Share Price Surge Justified Amid Oil Price Optimism in 2025?
data.description:
• Thinking about whether BP is actually a bargain or just riding on energy sector hype? Let’s get into what’s been driving its value and what that means for your next move.
  • BP's share price has gained 10.8% in the last month, 14.3% year-to-date, and delivered a whopping 142.0% return over five years, hinting at both renewed growth prospects and shifting sentiment around risk.
  • Much of this momentum has been fueled by recent news, including increased oil price optimism and industry-wide conversations about energy security and climate transition. International deals and ongoing discussions about BP’s strategic shift toward renewables have kept the spotlight on future growth versus traditional oil operations.
  • On our valuation checks, BP scores a 2 out of 6 (see full scorecard), suggesting there is room for a deeper dive into what those numbers mean. Let’s break down the main valuation approaches, and stick around to discover an even sharper way to think about BP’s value at the end of this article.

BP scores just 2/6 on our valuation checks. See what other red flags we found in the full valuation breakdown.

Advertisement

Approach 1: BP Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

The Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) method estimates a company’s intrinsic value by projecting future free cash flows and discounting them back to today’s value. This helps investors evaluate whether BP’s shares reflect its actual earning potential over time.

For BP, the latest twelve months' free cash flow stands at $11.12 Billion. Analyst estimates take this figure and project it into the future, with forecasts showing free cash flow reaching $10.95 Billion by 2029. Simply Wall St continues this projection out to 2035, based on reasonable growth assumptions.

Cash flow projections span billions of dollars each year, ranging from $10.45 Billion in 2026 to $12.33 Billion in 2035. Most of the nearer-term numbers (next five years) are based on consensus analyst estimates. Later years rely on trend-based forecasts.

When these future cash flows are discounted back to today and summed, BP’s estimated intrinsic value lands at $10.21 per share. At current prices, the DCF analysis suggests BP is trading at a 54.9% discount to this intrinsic value. This signals notable upside based on cash flow fundamentals.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests BP is undervalued by 54.9%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 879 more undervalued stocks based on cash flows.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for BP.

Approach 2: BP Price vs Earnings

For established, profitable companies like BP, the Price-to-Earnings (PE) ratio is a widely used tool to benchmark valuation. It provides a straightforward way for investors to see how much they're paying for each pound of BP’s earnings. This makes it especially meaningful for energy giants with stable profit streams.

Growth expectations play a big role in what counts as a “normal” PE. Higher expected growth or lower risk can justify higher multiples. On the other hand, low growth prospects or higher risks tend to pull the fair PE lower. BP’s current PE ratio is 61.3x, which stands out in comparison to its peer average of 11.7x and the broader oil and gas industry average of 13.6x.

Rather than just looking at industry averages or peers, Simply Wall St's proprietary “Fair Ratio” analyzes factors like BP’s earnings growth forecast, profit margins, market cap, risk profile, and industry context to offer a more tailored comparison. For BP, this Fair Ratio is 21.2x. This approach gives a clearer sense of what would be reasonable, factoring in unique company-specific traits rather than broad strokes.

Comparing BP’s actual PE of 61.3x to its Fair Ratio of 21.2x, the shares look significantly overvalued by this measure.

Result: OVERVALUED

PE ratios tell one story, but what if the real opportunity lies elsewhere? Discover 1405 companies where insiders are betting big on explosive growth.

Upgrade Your Decision Making: Choose your BP Narrative

Earlier we mentioned that there's an even better way to understand valuation, so let's introduce you to Narratives. A Narrative is your own simple, flexible “story” about BP. It connects what you believe about the company’s future (like its expected growth, margins, or risks) to a forecast of revenue and earnings, and then maps that to a fair value for the shares. Narratives make valuation more personal and practical: rather than just relying on a fixed consensus or formula, you build your view by adjusting assumptions that reflect your perspective and research.

These Narratives link BP’s real-world story, such as strategic deals, new discoveries, or climate transition plans, to the underlying numbers that actually drive fair value. This approach shows exactly how different events affect what the stock is really worth. On Simply Wall St’s Community page, millions of investors use Narratives to crowdsource insights, instantly see how new headlines change forecasts, and compare their own Fair Value to others' in a transparent and dynamic way.

For example, the most optimistic Narrative for BP currently factors in ambitious upstream growth and gives a fair value above £5.2, while the most cautious Narrative, focusing on transition risks and earnings volatility, lands closer to £3.82. With this tool, you can clearly see when your fair value suggests it’s time to buy, hold, or sell, and instantly update your thinking as new facts emerge.

Do you think there's more to the story for BP? Head over to our Community to see what others are saying!

data.title: Disc Medicine (IRON): Valuation Insights Following FDA Priority Review and New Clinical Trial Launch
data.description:
Disc Medicine (IRON) is making waves with two major updates. The company has launched a Phase 1b trial for its investigational therapy in sickle cell disease, and the FDA has granted priority review to its new drug application for bitopertin.

See our latest analysis for Disc Medicine.

Disc Medicine’s clinical and regulatory momentum appears to be fueling strong investor interest, with the stock delivering a robust 51.3% total shareholder return over the past year and a 50% share price return in the last 90 days alone. Short-term momentum has clearly picked up, and recent news around trial progress and priority review adds to the optimism about long-term growth prospects.

If the buzz around Disc Medicine’s new trial and FDA review has you watching the sector closely, now is a great opportunity to discover other healthcare stocks making headlines by exploring See the full list for free.

With Disc Medicine’s shares surging on clinical milestones and regulatory catalysts, the key question for investors is whether these gains leave room for more upside or if the market is already factoring in future growth.

Advertisement

Price-to-Book Ratio of 5.9x: Is it justified?

Disc Medicine currently trades at a price-to-book (P/B) ratio of 5.9x, using the last close price of $89.95. This level suggests investors are assigning a premium to Disc Medicine’s net asset value, compared both to the sector and to its closest peers.

The price-to-book ratio reflects what investors are willing to pay for each dollar of company assets. For biotech firms like Disc Medicine, this ratio can be especially significant because it incorporates market hopes for the company’s future scientific and commercial breakthroughs beyond its current book value.

At 5.9x, Disc Medicine’s P/B ratio is higher than the US Biotechs industry average of 2.5x. However, when comparing against its peer average of 102x, the company appears relatively attractively valued within its group. If the market were to shift closer to peer levels, significant upside could emerge. Relative to the industry average, though, the stock looks expensive on this metric.

See what the numbers say about this price — find out in our valuation breakdown.

Result: Price-to-Book of 5.9x (OVERVALUED compared to industry average, UNDERVALUED compared to peer average)

However, risks remain if clinical results disappoint or if competitive developments emerge. Either scenario could quickly shift sentiment and current momentum around Disc Medicine.

Another View: Discounted Cash Flow Perspective

Looking at Disc Medicine through our DCF model offers a different picture from the price-to-book valuation. The SWS DCF model estimates a fair value of $100.52, which is around 10.5% above the current share price. This suggests Disc Medicine could be undervalued based on the company's expected future cash flows. data.title: Why Tokyo Tatemono (TSE:8804) Is Up 13.4% After Reviewing Dividend and Share Cancellation Plans
data.description:
• Tokyo Tatemono recently held a board meeting on November 13, 2025, to consider revisions to its full-year consolidated financial results forecast, dividend forecast, and the possible cancellation of its own shares under Article 178 of the Companies Act.
  • This agenda signals the company's intent to actively manage its capital structure and shareholder returns, reflecting significant decisions that may shape future corporate direction and investor expectations.
  • We'll examine how the review of dividend policy and potential share cancellation could influence Tokyo Tatemono's investment narrative.

What Is Tokyo Tatemono's Investment Narrative?

For shareholders in Tokyo Tatemono, the big picture story is a mix of ongoing earnings momentum, frequent dividends, and tangible buybacks that reflect active stewardship. The board’s latest move to review forecasts, dividend policy, and the possible cancellation of shares is significant because it suggests the company may adjust its near-term outlook or capital return policy. This could have real implications for short-term catalysts such as dividend growth and share price performance, especially given strong price gains and sustained dividend hikes announced prior to this news. However, the headline risk remains: large one-off earnings items have recently impacted results, so revised forecasts could recalibrate growth expectations or highlight the need for further balance sheet discipline. For now, this board decision is a catalyst that might influence both perceived opportunity and risk in holding the stock, and it’s vital to watch how these board measures play out in upcoming disclosures.

But, beneath the company’s capital return moves, one-off earnings items remain a key risk that investors should watch. Tokyo Tatemono's shares are on the way up, but they could be overextended by 46%. Uncover the fair value now.

data.title: Dillard's (DDS): Evaluating Valuation After Recent Shareholder Return Surge and Revenue Decline
data.description:
Dillard's (DDS) shares have delivered strong returns for investors over the past year, rising nearly 58%. The company’s latest performance has caught the eye of the market, with recent gains outpacing broader retail peers.

See our latest analysis for Dillard's.

Dillard's momentum is hard to ignore, with a stunning 57.8% total shareholder return over the past year and a 23% share price gain in just three months. Price-to-Earnings of 17.1x: Is it justified?

Dillard's is trading at a price-to-earnings (P/E) ratio of 17.1x, which is lower than both its global industry peers and the wider US market. Despite the stock's sharp run-up, this multiple suggests the market is not assigning a premium for its current level of profitability.

The P/E ratio compares a company's share price to its earnings per share and is a classic gauge for how much investors are willing to pay for each dollar of profit. For a retailer like Dillard's, this matters because it reflects expectations for both stable performance and future growth, relative to alternatives in the same sector.

Right now, Dillard's P/E sits well below the global Multiline Retail industry average of 20.2x and the US market's 18.2x. This presents Dillard's as a potential value pick within retail. However, it is important to note that its P/E is still higher than the estimated fair P/E ratio of 10.7x. If the market focuses more on underlying profit sustainability rather than recent share price momentum, there is room for the multiple to shift towards that fair value level.

However, weaker annual revenue and net income raise concerns that market optimism could fade if fundamentals continue to retreat.

data.title: Will Analyst Skepticism on Alixorexton Reshape Alkermes' (ALKS) Competitive Strategy in Narcolepsy?
data.description:
• Alkermes recently announced positive topline results from its Vibrance-2 phase 2 study of alixorexton, an investigational oral orexin 2 receptor agonist, in patients with narcolepsy type 2, showing statistically significant improvements in wakefulness and excessive daytime sleepiness compared to placebo.
  • Despite meeting primary trial endpoints and presenting a favorable safety profile, the results drew mixed analyst feedback regarding the drug's differentiation versus competitors, especially as new data continues to shape expectations for this therapeutic class.
  • We'll examine how investor concerns about alixorexton's competitive positioning influence Alkermes' broader investment narrative following these recent clinical trial results.

Alkermes Investment Narrative Recap

To be a shareholder in Alkermes, you need to believe in the company’s ability to translate its neuroscience pipeline, especially proprietary orexin agonists, into commercial success that can offset margin pressures from rising R&D costs and expiring patents. The recent positive Vibrance-2 study results for alixorexton address a key pipeline catalyst, but concerns about differentiation from competitors and the muted stock reaction suggest the outcome does not materially de-risk the company’s short-term commercial outlook or its biggest risks around future revenue and earnings growth. The upcoming presentation of new alixorexton data at the SLEEP 2025 conference is especially relevant. This event will give investors more insight into efficacy and safety details that influence late-stage trial prospects, one of the most closely watched pipeline catalysts following the mixed analyst feedback around competitive positioning and market uptake. data.title: Monster Beverage’s (MNST) Earnings Jump Raises Fresh Questions About Its 2024 Buyback Pause
data.description:
• Monster Beverage Corporation recently reported third quarter 2025 results, showing sales of US$2.20 billion and net income of US$524.46 million, both higher than the prior year, while confirming no shares were repurchased under its 2024 buyback program during the quarter.
  • The company's strong growth in sales and net income highlights robust demand and operational execution despite a pause in buyback activity.
  • We'll explore how Monster Beverage's significant year-over-year earnings growth informs its investment narrative and future prospects.

This technology could replace computers: discover 26 stocks that are working to make quantum computing a reality.

Monster Beverage Investment Narrative Recap

To be a Monster Beverage shareholder, you need to believe in the continued global momentum for functional and energy drinks, supported by consistent innovation and international expansion. While the company’s robust third quarter results affirm strong top-line growth, the recent news showing a pause in share repurchases does not materially affect the most important near-term catalyst, demand for new product launches and margin management. The biggest current risk remains potential margin pressure from rising costs and shifts in international sales mix, neither of which was directly altered by the buyback update.

The most relevant recent announcement is Monster's third quarter earnings release, which revealed substantial year-over-year net income and revenue growth. These figures reinforce the strength of Monster's underlying demand and international activities, contextualizing ongoing catalysts like its low and zero-sugar offerings and global distribution partnerships as engines of further revenue diversification and scale. However, it’s important to remember that despite this momentum, the company continues to face underlying cost risks that could impact future performance.

In contrast, investors should also be aware that gross margin improvements may face renewed pressure if commodity costs or tariffs rise faster than Monster’s ability to pass on higher prices to consumers...

Monster Beverage's narrative projects $9.8 billion revenue and $2.5 billion earnings by 2028. This requires 8.5% yearly revenue growth and a $0.9 billion earnings increase from $1.6 billion.
